intro: Puccinellia vaginata, also known as Vernal grass or Sea Sand-grass, is a perennial grass species native to coastal regions of eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Puccinellia vaginata include sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Puccinellia vaginata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
